prompts.abstract: The object is an L-shaped CAD model with a vertical section and a horizontal section forming a right angle. The vertical section is longer than the horizontal section.
prompts.description: The object is an L-shaped CAD model with a vertical section and a horizontal section forming a right angle. The vertical section is longer than the horizontal section.
prompts.beginner: The design features a rectangular block with a small cylindrical hole and several cylindrical sections of varying heights. The block serves as the base, with the cylindrical sections adding structural complexity and functionality.
prompts.intermediate: The design consists of a rectangular block with a series of cylindrical sections and a hole. The rectangular block has a length of approximately 0.118, a width of 0.007, and a height of 0.042. A cylindrical hole is drilled into the block, with a diameter of 0.011 and a depth of 0.056. The vertical cylindrical section stands tall with a height of 0.639, while the horizontal cylindrical section has a height of 0.417. The final cylindrical section is the tallest, with a height of 0.75. These sections are all aligned and integrated into the rectangular block to form the final complex shape.
prompts.expert:
Create a rectangular block with holes by first setting up a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to zero degrees and a translation vector of [0.0069, 0.0069, 0.0]. On the first face, draw a 2D sketch consisting of a loop with six lines and two arcs. The first line starts at (0.0, 0.0) and ends at (0.1181, 0.0). The second line continues from (0.1181, 0.0) to (0.1181, 0.0069). The third line goes from (0.1181, 0.0069) to (0.0861, 0.0069). The first arc starts at (0.0861, 0.0069), passes through (0.0833, 0.0042), and ends at (0.0806, 0.0069). The fourth line extends from (0.0806, 0.0069) to (0.0375, 0.0069). The second arc begins at (0.0375, 0.0069), passes through (0.0347, 0.0042), and ends at (0.0319, 0.0069). The fifth line runs from (0.0319, 0.0069) to (0.0, 0.0069). Finally, the sixth line completes the loop by connecting (0.0, 0.0069) back to (0.0, 0.0). Scale this sketch by a factor of 0.1181, then transform it into 3D by rotating with Euler angles [0.0, 0.0, 0.0] and translating using the vector [0.0069, 0.0069, 0.0]. Extrude the sketch 0.0417 units along the normal to create a new solid body. The dimensions of the block are length 0.11805555555555554, width 0.006944444444444442, and height 0.041666666666666664.

Next, construct a cylindrical hole by creating a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to [180.0, 0.0, -90.0] degrees and a translation vector of [0.0716, 0.0139, 0.0152]. On the first face, draw a circle with a center at (0.0056, 0.0056) and a radius of 0.0056. Scale this sketch by a factor of 0.0112, then transform it into 3D by rotating with Euler angles [180.0, 0.0, -90.0] and translating using the vector [0.0716, 0.0139, 0.0152]. Extrude the sketch 0.0556 units in the opposite direction to remove material from the existing body. The dimensions of the cylindrical hole are length 0.011166666666666665, width 0.011166666666666665, and height 0.05555555555555555.

For the vertical cylindrical section, set up a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to zero degrees and a translation vector of [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]. On the first face, draw a circle with a center at (0.0069, 0.0069) and a radius of 0.0069. Scale this sketch by a factor of 0.0139, then transform it into 3D by rotating with Euler angles [0.0, 0.0, 0.0] and translating using the vector [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]. Extrude the sketch 0.6389 units along the normal to create a new solid body. The dimensions of the vertical cylindrical section are length 0.013888888888888886, width 0.013888888888888886, and height 0.6388888888888888.

To construct the horizontal cylindrical section, set up a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to zero degrees and a translation vector of [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]. On the first face, draw a circle with a center at (0.0069, 0.0069) and a radius of 0.0069. Scale this sketch by a factor of 0.0139, then transform it into 3D by rotating with Euler angles [0.0, 0.0, 0.0] and translating using the vector [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]. Extrude the sketch 0.4167 units along the normal to create a new solid body. The dimensions of the horizontal cylindrical section are length 0.013888888888888886, width 0.013888888888888886, and height 0.4166666666666667.

Finally, construct the final cylindrical section by setting up a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to zero degrees and a translation vector of [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]. On the first face, draw a circle with a center at (0.0069, 0.0069) and a radius of 0.0069. Scale this sketch by a factor of 0.0139, then transform it into 3D by rotating with Euler angles [0.0, 0.0, 0.0] and translating using the vector [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]. Extrude the sketch 0.75 units along the normal to create a new solid body. The dimensions of the final cylindrical section are length 0.013888888888888886, width 0.013888888888888886, and height 0.75.

prompts.nli_data:
<part_1>
### Construct a Rectangular Block with Holes

#### Create a New Coordinate System
- Set Euler Angles to [0.0, 0.0, 0.0] degrees.
- Set Translation Vector to [0.0069, 0.0069, 0.0].

#### Draw a 2D Sketch
- **Face 1**
  - **Loop 1**
    - **Line 1**: Start at (0.0, 0.0), end at (0.1181, 0.0).
    - **Line 2**: Start at (0.1181, 0.0), end at (0.1181, 0.0069).
    - **Line 3**: Start at (0.1181, 0.0069), end at (0.0861, 0.0069).
    - **Arc 1**: Start at (0.0861, 0.0069), mid at (0.0833, 0.0042), end at (0.0806, 0.0069).
    - **Line 4**: Start at (0.0806, 0.0069), end at (0.0375, 0.0069).
    - **Arc 2**: Start at (0.0375, 0.0069), mid at (0.0347, 0.0042), end at (0.0319, 0.0069).
    - **Line 5**: Start at (0.0319, 0.0069), end at (0.0, 0.0069).
    - **Line 6**: Start at (0.0, 0.0069), end at (0.0, 0.0).

#### Scale the 2D Sketch
- Apply a scale factor of 0.1181.

#### Transform the Scaled 2D Sketch into 3D
- Rotate using Euler Angles [0.0, 0.0, 0.0] degrees.
- Translate using the vector [0.0069, 0.0069, 0.0].

#### Extrude the 2D Sketch
- Extrude 0.0417 units towards the normal.
- Do not extrude in the opposite direction.
- Create a new solid body.

#### Dimensions
- Length: 0.11805555555555554
- Width: 0.006944444444444442
- Height: 0.041666666666666664

---

<part_2>
### Construct a Cylindrical Hole

#### Create a New Coordinate System
- Set Euler Angles to [180.0, 0.0, -90.0] degrees.
- Set Translation Vector to [0.0716, 0.0139, 0.0152].

#### Draw a 2D Sketch
- **Face 1**
  - **Loop 1**
    - **Circle 1**: Center at (0.0056, 0.0056), Radius 0.0056.

#### Scale the 2D Sketch
- Apply a scale factor of 0.0112.

#### Transform the Scaled 2D Sketch into 3D
- Rotate using Euler Angles [180.0, 0.0, -90.0] degrees.
- Translate using the vector [0.0716, 0.0139, 0.0152].

#### Extrude the 2D Sketch
- Do not extrude towards the normal.
- Extrude 0.0556 units in the opposite direction.
- Remove material from the existing body.

#### Dimensions
- Length: 0.011166666666666665
- Width: 0.011166666666666665
- Height: 0.05555555555555555

---
